frameworks.name: perceptual decision-making with metacognitive confidence
frameworks.one_line: single-neuron analysis testing whether SC encodes decision versus decision confidence

frameworks.name: decision-confidence dissociation paradigm
frameworks.one_line: task design that orthogonalizes perceptual evidence for choice from evidence for confidence

frameworks.name: buildup vs burst neuron taxonomy (superior colliculus)
frameworks.one_line: classification of SC neurons by prelude activity used to stratify single-unit analyses

core_question: Do individual superior colliculus neurons carry signals reflecting decision confidence beyond decision accuracy?
key_assumptions: decision accuracy and reported confidence are normally correlated and must be dissociated
SC neuronal subtypes (buildup vs non-buildup) may carry distinct decision-related signals
manipulations: task dissociating perceptual information for the decision from perceptual information for the confidence report
stratification of SC neurons by prelude activity (buildup vs non-buildup)
key_findings: when accuracy and confidence covaried, modulation arose primarily in buildup neurons
after isolating confidence from accuracy, only a few neurons (mostly buildup) showed unique confidence-related signals
effect sizes for unique confidence coding were very small
interpretations: single SC neurons encode the decision itself rather than decision confidence
prior population-level confidence signals were largely inherited from accuracy covariation
